free_text: 36 MeV triton inelastic scattering and one-nucleon
Middleton-type sputter source, in which tritium is
liberated from a titanium cone by a caesium beam.
Beam intensities up to 600 nA on target were obtained.
Average beam current was about 200 nA.
Target thicknesses were measured by 25 MeV alpha
scattering experiments and confirmed by forward-angle
telescopes were mounted on each of two independently
rotating arms. Delta-E detectors were between 118
microns and 155 microns thick with E-detectors being
5 mm thick. These were cooled to improve energy
Scattering angle could be set with accuracy of better
